summary: DHH celebrates the Supreme Court's ruling striking down affirmative action in college admissions, framing it as part of a broader decline of DEI ideology that he sees as overdue. He argues that the 2020-2021 period represented peak DEI influence, particularly in tech, and that the pendulum is now swinging back. He contends that using racial preferences to correct past injustices amounts to present-day discrimination, particularly against Asian Americans. He predicts the ruling will accelerate DEI's decline in corporate settings and sees it as a vindication of meritocratic ideals.
key_points: The Supreme Court's affirmative action ruling generated a surprisingly contained reaction in tech, which DHH attributes to DEI's already-waning influence in the industry
The 2020-2021 peak of DEI ideology is compared to the 1950s Red Scare in its intensity and eventual reassessment
Corporate legal departments are already course-correcting in anticipation of lawsuits extending the ruling's logic beyond college admissions
Asian Americans bore disproportionate penalties under affirmative action, making the system's discriminatory nature most visible
While DEI is receding in tech and corporate worlds, academia, arts, and science remain in its grip
money_quotes: Correcting past injustices with present-day discrimination was never going to be a long-term stable project.

When you attempt to rig the rules to achieve such 'demographically proportionate' outcomes, you invariably end up penalizing better candidates on the basis of having the 'wrong', 'over-represented' racial background. That's not right.
It really was a crazy time. And I think we're only beginning to appreciate just how bananas it was.
With the legal basis drained, the ideological nature of this push will become all the more apparent and niche at the same time.
The original American promise that this is a country where you can make it regardless of where you came from has always been challenged in practice, but it's a beautiful ideal.
Using race, and other protected identity markers, to advantage some candidates over others is illegal.
key_insight: The Supreme Court's affirmative action ruling marks a legal turning point that will accelerate DEI's decline from corporate and institutional life, vindicating merit-based evaluation over racial preferences.
